Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Lord Rama

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-12-13 · 3 answers
Hare Krishna Pr,
How did lord Rama descend to the spiritual world at the time of ending his earthly pastime?
Any scriptural evidence for the same?
user [449] · 2009-12-13
hare krishna pr I got the answer form Ramayana of Krishna Dharma pr
Lord Rama and his two brothers merged in Vishnu who appeared on the back of Garuda.

I just wanted to ask whether my following opinion is right r wrong?

" Theres no avatara except Krishna who while ascending to the spiritual world left some body.
This itself proves Krishnas supremacy in a way that although He is Supreme Personality of Godhead,
he displayed his qualities of
1] Renunciation: to make the word of his devotees true (Narada Muni and Gandhari) he did this last pastime.
2] Mercifulness: he gave an chance to athestic people that they will say Krishna is not God."
user [154] · 2009-12-16
Does not make sense. Krishna is just having more fun...
user [149] · 2009-12-16
onkar: Theres no avatara except Krishna who while ascending to the spiritual world left some body.

No. According to the Vishnu purana, Sri Balarama also left a body.
